purpose: Reusable real-image source library for Corporate Shadows. Prefer these archival photographs, paintings, maps, documents, and public-domain collection pages over generated cards wherever possible.
global_rules: Prefer real archival images over generated graphics for historical claims, people, locations, products, maps, courtrooms, factories, and documents.
Keep generated graphics for abstract concepts only: psychological manipulation, market structure, invisible cartel rules, or chart/table moments.
Generated visual holds should target 8 seconds and should not exceed 10 seconds whenever practical.
Complete the first five Corporate Shadows videos with this real-image workflow before expanding into Omni Flash or AI-video-heavy production.
If AI video is used later, use it as short supplemental clips only, not as the documentary backbone.
Verify each file page license before publishing. Commons category pages are discovery pools, not automatic license approval.
Record every selected file in asset_attribution.json with source_url, title, author/repository, license, and credit.
Use source diversity: do not repeat one archival image for more than 10 seconds unless the narration is intentionally pausing on a key reveal.
